content.full_text:
MONTGOMERY
WARD

LAW AND GOVERNMENT AFFAIRS DIVISION

Regional Counsel
Irwin J. Shapiro

January 9,1979

General Attorneys
Gordon E. Hepler
John M. Hunter
Francis J. Stifman


Ms. Barbara Campbell
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA.
95804

Dear Ms. Campbell:

This is confirmation of our telephone conversation of January 8, 1979
relative to the filing of Form 461 by Parent/Subsidiary Corporations.

It is my understanding that Montgomery Ward and Co.,Inc. and fully
owned subsidiary of Mobil Corporation, may file one form 461 for
the political contribution made to the Retailer's Good Government
Council. Further, it is my understanding that if it is noted that
Montgomery Ward is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Mobil Corporation,
and that Mobil Corporation approved the contribution on the form
461, the filing by Montgomery Ward and Co., Inc. will fill the
requirements of In re Kahn found at 2 FPPC OPS 151.

Thank you for taking the time to discuss this matter with me.

John M. Hunter
General Attorney

JMH:jo


REGIONAL OFFICE 2825 EAST 14TH STREET / OAKLAND / CALIFORNIA 94616 / PHONE 533-1300

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Montgomery Ward and Co., Inc., as a wholly-owned subsidiary of Mobil Corporation, may file a single Form 461 for a political contribution made to the Retailer's Good Government Council.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Montgomery Ward and Co., Inc. may file one Form 461 for the political contribution if it notes that it is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Mobil Corporation and that Mobil Corporation approved the contribution, which will satisfy the requirements of In re Kahn (2 FPPC OPS 151).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming correspondence/request from an external party (Mobil/Montgomery Ward) to the FPPC, not an FPPC advice letter response. The document confirms a prior telephone conversation and contains an understanding of filing requirements rather than formal FPPC guidance. No formal question/conclusion sections are present.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Montgomery Ward and Co., Inc., as a wholly-owned subsidiary of Mobil Corporation, may file a single Form 461 for a political contribution made to the Retailer's Good Government Council.

CONCLUSION: Montgomery Ward and Co., Inc. may file one Form 461 for the political contribution if it notes that it is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Mobil Corporation and that Mobil Corporation approved the contribution, which will satisfy the requirements of In re Kahn (2 FPPC OPS 151).

It is my understanding that Montgomery Ward and Co.,Inc. and fully owned subsidiary of Mobil Corporation, may file one form 461 for the political contribution made to the Retailer's Good Government Council. Further, it is my understanding that if it is noted that Montgomery Ward is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Mobil Corporation, and that Mobil Corporation approved the contribution on the form 461, the filing by Montgomery Ward and Co., Inc. will fill the requirements of In re Kahn found at 2 FPPC OPS 151. embedding.summary: This is a confirmation letter from John M. Hunter, General Attorney, to Ms. Barbara Campbell at the FPPC, confirming a telephone discussion regarding Form 461 filing requirements for a parent corporation and its wholly-owned subsidiary concerning a political contribution.
